title: Lazy Peach Ferment Curry
description: A spicy, crunchy Moroccan-inspired dish featuring peach and ferment, suitable for lazy Sundays and jain-friendly diets.

ingredients.name: 1 ripe peach
ingredients.notes: Choose a peach that is ripe but not mushy.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons coconut milk
ingredients.notes: Use full-fat coconut milk for richness and creaminess.

ingredients.name: 1 tablespoon olive oil
ingredients.notes: Ensure the temperature of the pan is at safe cooking temperatures before adding the oil.

ingredients.name: 1 teaspoon ground cumin
ingredients.notes: Add more to taste for a spicier dish.

ingredients.name: 1/2 teaspoon chili powder
ingredients.notes: Increase this amount if you prefer a hotter curry.

ingredients.name: Juice of 1 lime
ingredients.notes: Use fresh lime juice for the best flavor and health benefits.

ingredients.name: Salt to taste
ingredients.notes: A pinch should be sufficient; adjust to your liking.

instructions: Cut the peach into bite-sized pieces.
In a pan, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add cumin and chili powder, stirring for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the peach pieces and coconut milk. Stir well to combine.
Simmer gently for about 5-7 minutes or until the mixture thickens slightly and the peaches are tender.
Remove from heat, stir in lime juice and salt. Mix thoroughly and serve hot as a side dish or main course.
Optional: Garnish with fresh cilantro or mint leaves.
